question_title: What will be the value of "4-Week Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate, Discount Basis" on 2026-04-09?
summary: The 4-week Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate (DTB4WK) on April 9, 2026 is forecast to remain very close to its current value of 3.64%. The Fed funds target range is stable at 3.50-3.75%, rate futures imply 3.64 through July with no rate change expected, and the series showed only a 3bp range (3.62-3.65) throughout all of March 2026. With no FOMC meeting anticipated before April 9, the rate should continue its extremely stable behavior. Monte Carlo simulation using empirical daily change distributions (mostly 0 or ±1bp) produces a tight distribution centered at 3.64 with about 98% probability within ±5bp.

factors.description: Current value is 3.64 as of March 31, 2026 - most recent observation and strongest anchor for this policy-linked rate
Fed funds futures imply 3.64 for April, May, June, July - no rate change expected before resolution
Fed funds target range stable at 3.50-3.75 throughout March, no FOMC meeting expected before April 9
Extremely low daily volatility (σ=0.007) and tight March range (3.62-3.65, only 3bp) indicate minimal day-to-day variation
Potential tail risk from surprise macro events, tax season money market effects, or emergency Fed action could cause larger moves

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary

**Question**: What will be the value of the 4-Week Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate on 2026-04-09?

**Median forecast: 3.64%** (same as current value)

### Key Evidence
- **Current value**: 3.64% as of March 31, 2026
- **Fed funds rate**: Stable at 3.50-3.75% target range, unchanged all March
- **Rate futures**: Imply 3.64% for April through July — no rate change expected
- **March volatility**: Extremely low — entire month's range was only 3bp (3.62-3.65)
- **Forecast horizon**: ~7 business days with no FOMC meeting expected

### Distribution
| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| P10 | 3.62 |
| P25 | 3.63 |
| P50 | 3.64 |
| P75 | 3.65 |
| P90 | 3.67 |

This is a very tight distribution because the 4-week T-bill rate is policy-linked and barely moves between FOMC meetings. The small tails (P1=3.59, P99=3.70) account for low-probability disruptions like market stress or surprise policy signals.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) What will be the value of "4-Week Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate, Discount Basis" on 2026-04-09?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42992)
[4-Week Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate, Discount Basis (DTB4WK) | FRED | St. Louis Fed](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DTB4WK)
[Federal Funds Target Range - Upper Limit (DFEDTARU) | FRED | St. Louis Fed](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DFEDTARU)
resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series DTB4WK once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=DTB4WK`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-04-09 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.

factors.description: Current value is 3.64 as of March 31, 2026 - this is the most recent observation and the strongest anchor
Fed funds futures imply 3.64 for April, May, June, July - no rate change expected
Fed funds target range stable at 3.50-3.75 throughout March, no FOMC meeting before April 9
Extremely low daily volatility (0.007) and tight March range (3.62-3.65, only 3bp)
Potential tail risk from surprise macro events, market stress, or emergency Fed action

input.anchor: Current value: 3.64, confirmed by FRED API data and rate futures for April 2026
input.assessment: The 4-week T-bill rate is extremely stable between FOMC meetings. With no policy change expected before April 9 (confirmed by Fed funds futures at 3.64 through July), the rate should remain within a very tight range around its current value of 3.64. The entire March range was only 3bp (3.62-3.65). Monte Carlo simulation using empirical daily change distributions produces a very tight distribution centered at 3.64, with ~57% probability within ±2bp and ~98% within ±5bp. The main risk is a tail event (emergency Fed action, market crisis) which would be unprecedented in the current stable environment.
input.calibration_notes: Distribution is very tight because this is a policy-linked rate that barely moves between FOMC meetings. The March data shows daily changes are almost always 0 or ±1bp. Rate futures confirm no change expected. My P10-P90 range of 3.62-3.67 is wider than the entire March trading range (3.62-3.65) which seems appropriate for a 7-business-day horizon.
input.key_uncertainties: 1. Whether there's an FOMC meeting before April 9 (very unlikely based on typical 6-7 week spacing). 2. Whether a surprise macro event could shift money market rates. 3. Tax season effects on T-bill demand (April 15 is close).
input.tool_audit: FRED API via web_search: Excellent - provided full March observations, regime stats, and rate futures. Fed funds target range data: Confirmed 3.75 stable all month. FOMC schedule search: Inconclusive - couldn't find definitive 2026 schedule, but June 16-17 meeting confirmed. fetch_url for FRED: Failed with internal server error. fetch_url for equals money: Failed (no archived snapshot).
input.process_reflection: This was a clean, well-supported forecast. The FRED API data through web_search was excellent, providing not just observations but regime stats and rate futures - exactly what's needed. The main friction was difficulty finding the 2026 FOMC calendar despite multiple search attempts. The discrete Monte Carlo approach works well for this type of rounded, policy-linked rate.
